summary: Iteration 8: Tested Task 3 injury swap logic (SI Joint / Pelvis), auth flows, profile isolation, coach/PR endpoints. Found 2 critical data-isolation bugs in /api/profile and injury_preview fallback. Auth flows all pass. apply-injury-update does not crash and returns exercises_swapped. Trap Bar Deadlift correctly NOT restricted for SI Joint.

critical.issue: Not user-scoped. Uses `find_one({})` with NO userId filter — returns the same default profile document for ALL authenticated users. user_a and user_b both get identical profile data. profile POST/PUT also use `find_one({})` without userId.

Fallback on line 300 `if not profile: profile = await db.profile.find_one({})` inherits default user's injuryFlags when new user has no profile. Default user has injuryFlags=['SI Joint / Pelvis'] so fresh users appear to 'already have' SI Joint — addedInjuries=[] instead of ['SI Joint / Pelvis']. Makes injury-preview useless for new users.

minor.issue: Returns exercises_swapped=0 and added=[] for user_a because user_a already had SI Joint / Pelvis from prior test run. Endpoint does NOT crash — works correctly. But data persisted in user-scoped profile is not reflected by GET /api/profile (returns default profile instead).

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix GET/POST/PUT /api/profile to be user-scoped. Add `userId: str = Depends(get_current_user)` to all profile endpoints and filter MongoDB queries with {'userId': userId}. Use upsert on POST/PUT with userId filter.

CRITICAL: Remove or fix the injury_preview fallback `if not profile: profile = await db.profile.find_one({})`. For new users with no profile, use empty defaults: `current_injuries = []`. Do NOT inherit default user data.

MEDIUM: The default user profile (userId: None) in MongoDB has injuryFlags=['SI Joint / Pelvis'] left over from testing — this is the root cause of the fallback contamination. Seed/reset the default profile or ensure it has no injuries.
Verify that apply-injury-update is actually persisting to user-scoped profile document. The upsert logic (line 364) looks correct but cannot be verified since GET /profile is broken.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py lines 193-221: GET/POST/PUT /api/profile all use find_one({}) without userId — no user scoping at all. This is a data isolation failure.
server.py line 300: `if not profile: profile = await db.profile.find_one({})` — dangerous fallback that corrupts new-user injury baseline with default user data.
coach/conversations endpoint (line 946) hardcodes userId='default' — not user-scoped.

PASSED: POST /api/plan/injury-preview Trap Bar NOT restricted for SI Joint — CORRECT
GET /api/plan/session/today user_a — 404 (no plan, expected for incomplete onboarding)
FAILED: GET /api/profile user_a — profile has no 'email' field (returns onboarding data, not user account)
Data isolation — user_a and user_b return identical profile (both get default find_one({}) result)
POST /api/plan/injury-preview SI Joint restricted exercises — fresh user inherits default user's SI Joint injury flag via fallback, so addedInjuries=[] and exercisesRestricted=[]
Injury flags persisted in profile — profile.injuries empty (field is 'injuryFlags' not 'injuries', and GET /profile not user-scoped)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Profile endpoints completely lack userId scoping. The injury-preview fallback to find_one({}) is the root cause of SI Joint test failures. After fixing profile scoping, re-run test_iteration8.py — the injury-preview tests need a fresh user with no prior injuries. user_a now has injuryFlags=['SI Joint / Pelvis'] in their user-scoped profile document (from apply-injury-update).
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: GET/POST/PUT /api/profile endpoints use `db.profile.find_one({})` without any userId filter. This means: (1) all users share the same profile document, (2) injury_preview baseline for new users is polluted with default user's injuryFlags, (3) apply-injury-update correctly upserts with userId but GET /profile never returns that user-scoped document. Fix: add userId to all profile endpoint queries and upserts.
